execution_history.summary: 1 finding: keyboard detail scrolling is broken after the collapsible-section refactor. In [src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/agent-runs/B0069-review/repo/src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py#L564), `detail_max_scroll()` still derives scroll bounds from the old flat `format_detail_panel()` text, while the UI now renders `Collapsible` widgets and scrolls the `VerticalScroll` container in [_sync_detail_scroll](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/agent-runs/B0069-review/repo/src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py#L1499). In a manual repro with a long bead, pressing `End` set `runtime_offset` to 76 while `panel_scroll_y` and `panel_max_scroll_y` both stayed `0.0`, so expanded section content remains unreachable. The new regression at [tests/test_tui.py](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/agent-runs/B0069-review/repo/tests/test_tui.py#L1054) only checks the runtime offset, so it misses the visible regression.
Created corrective bead B0069-review-corrective for blocked issue
Requeued blocked bead after corrective bead B0069-review-corrective completed

No unresolved findings. Reviewed corrective commit 221de46 for B0069; the detail-panel scroll fix now clamps against the rendered VerticalScroll height, the README documents the behavior, and `python -m unittest tests.test_tui -q` passed with 62 tests.

guardrails.template_text:
# Review Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Inspect code, tests, docs, and acceptance criteria for correctness, completeness, and risk.

Allowed actions:
- Review changed files and call out bugs, regressions, missing tests, and documentation gaps.
- Validate acceptance criteria against the implementation and handoff state.
- Block with a clear recommendation when the bead actually requires implementation work.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature work, tests, or docs instead of reporting findings.
- Rewrite architecture or silently fix issues discovered during review.
- Mark incomplete work as accepted without evidence.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the review signoff decision: `approved` means the bead can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when no unresolved findings remain.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved finding count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when any required fix remains.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Review findings ordered by severity, or an explicit statement that no findings were discovered.
- Clear blocked handoff details when the task belongs to another agent type.

worker_prompt_context.title: Review Add collapsible sections in Details panel
worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks: This bead reviews shared TUI behavior in src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py and its regression coverage in tests/test_tui.py; the follow-up fix will touch the same panel-rendering and scroll-handling paths, so downstream TUI work should rebase carefully.

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed the B0069 implementation in README.md, src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py, and tests/test_tui.py against commit 024b2aa. Ran `python -m unittest tests.test_tui -q`; all 61 tests passed. Manually exercised the Textual app to verify detail scrolling behavior and reproduced the scroll mismatch introduced by the collapsible detail layout.
handoff_summary.remaining: Implementation follow-up is required in the TUI detail scroll logic. The scroll bounds need to be computed from the rendered detail container state rather than the legacy flattened text representation, and regression coverage should assert actual `VerticalScroll` movement or reachable expanded content.
handoff_summary.risks: Operators can expand a long section and still be unable to reach its contents with `j`, `PageDown`, or `End`, which undermines the core purpose of the collapsible detail panel and can hide acceptance criteria, file lists, or handoff data.
handoff_summary.next_action: Fix detail-panel scroll accounting to follow the rendered collapsible layout, then add a regression that proves the visible detail viewport actually scrolls when sections are expanded.
handoff_summary.block_reason: The collapsible detail-panel implementation regresses keyboard scrolling: runtime scroll offsets change, but the rendered `VerticalScroll` viewport does not move, so long expanded section content is inaccessible.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: This bead reviews shared TUI behavior in src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py and its regression coverage in tests/test_tui.py; the follow-up fix will touch the same panel-rendering and scroll-handling paths, so downstream TUI work should rebase carefully.

last_agent_result.summary: No unresolved findings. Reviewed corrective commit 221de46 for B0069; the detail-panel scroll fix now clamps against the rendered VerticalScroll height, the README documents the behavior, and `python -m unittest tests.test_tui -q` passed with 62 tests.
